name: SX127x Component
description: The SX127x component allows you to configure the SX1276, SX1277, SX1278 and SX1279 transceivers ([datasheet](https://www.semtech.com/products/wireless-rf/lora-connect/sx1278#documentation)) in ESPHome. Transceivers are connected via the [SPI Bus](https://esphome.io/components/spi). Supported frequencies range from 137 MHz to 1020 MHz, including the popular 315 MHz, 433 MHz, 868 MHz, and 915 MHz ISM bands. Supported modulations include LoRa, OOK, FSK, GFSK, MSK and GMSK. The SX127x component may be used as a platform for the [Packet Transport Component](https://esphome.io/components/packet_transport#packet-transport) component, enabling sensor data to be sent directly from one ESPHome node to another.

config_entries.label: CS Pin
config_entries.description: SPI chip select pin.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: DIO0 Pin
config_entries.description: Digital IO pin 0.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Frequency
config_entries.description: Frequency in Hz of the transceiver.

config_entries.label: Modulation
config_entries.description: Modulation can be `OOK`, `FSK` or `LORA`  `.

config_entries.label: RST Pin
config_entries.description: Reset pin.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Auto Cal
config_entries.description: Enable automatic image calibration on temperature changes. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Bitrate
config_entries.description: Bit rate of the signal. Required in packet mode and recommended in continuous mode. Normally the inverse of the bit duration, eg 1 / 208 us is 4800 bps.

config_entries.label: Bitsync
config_entries.description: Enables the receive bit synchronizer. Required in packet mode. Recommended in continuous mode, however if there is no preamble plus high noise it may be better to turn it off.

config_entries.label: Coding Rate
config_entries.description: Coding rate, values can be `CR_4_5`, `CR_4_6`, `CR_4_7` or `CR_4_8`. Defaults to `CR_4_5`.

config_entries.label: CRC Enable
config_entries.description: Enables a 16 bit CRC calculation/check in packet mode.

config_entries.label: Deviation
config_entries.description: Transmitter FSK frequency deviation, values range from 0 to 100 kHz. Defaults to `5kHz`.

config_entries.label: PA Pin
config_entries.description: Transmitter output pin, can be `BOOST` or `RFO`.

config_entries.label: PA Power
config_entries.description: Transmitter power, range is from 2 to 17 dBm when `pa_pin` is `BOOST` and 0 to 15 dBm when using `RFO`.

config_entries.label: Packet Mode
config_entries.description: In packet mode bytes are sent via the `send_packet` automation and received with the `on_packet` trigger. If not enabled continuous mode is used and raw data is sent and received on DIO2.

config_entries.label: Payload Length
config_entries.description: Length of the payload in packet mode. If not configured then the variable length packet format is used. Maximum length is 64.

config_entries.label: Preamble Detect
config_entries.description: Minimum length of the preamble in bytes required by the receiver. Preamble detector is disabled if the size is 0 and its value capped at 3.

config_entries.label: Preamble Errors
config_entries.description: Number of chip errors tolerated in the receiver.

config_entries.label: Preamble Polarity
config_entries.description: Polarity of the preamble, either 0xAA or 0x55.

config_entries.label: Preamble Size
config_entries.description: Length of the preamble, in bytes, to be sent by the transmitter. This value should be larger than `preamble_detect` on the receive side to allow time for the receiver’s AFC and AGC to adjust.

config_entries.label: RX Floor
config_entries.description: When receiving OOK `rx_floor` should be set appropriately for your environment. If set too high (ie closer to 0) the radio will ignore everything. If set too low (ie closer to -128) too much noise will get through. When receiving FSK without a preamble configured `rx_floor` is used to trigger the receiver.

config_entries.label: RX Start
config_entries.description: Start the receiver automatically on boot or after transmitting.

config_entries.label: Shaping
config_entries.description: Transmitter data shaping. In OOK can be `CUTOFF_BR_X_2`, `CUTOFF_BR_X_1` or `NONE`. In FSK can be `GAUSSIAN_BT_0_3`, `GAUSSIAN_BT_0_5`, `GAUSSIAN_BT_1_0` or `NONE`. Not recommended in continuous mode as the data on DIO2 must to be synchronized with the bit clock on DIO1.

config_entries.label: Spreading Factor
config_entries.description: Spreading factor, values range from 6 to 12. Defaults to 7.

config_entries.label: Sync Value
config_entries.description: Synchronization bytes, list of 1 to 8 bytes, found after the preamble and before the payload.
